headline: TeamViewer Appoints Peter Ruchatz as Chief Marketing Officer
summary: TeamViewer today announced the appointment of Peter Ruchatz as Chief Marketing Officer (CMO), effective September 2026. Ruchatz is an accomplished B2B software marketing leader with a proven track record of delivering strong growth in Enterprise and SMB segments. He will join TeamViewer's Senior Leadership Team and lead the global marketing organization across brand, product marketing, demand generation, channel marketing, and pricing.

headline: CVS Health to Deliver Faster, More Personalized Call Center Care for Millions of Members with Salesforce's Agentforce Health

headline: CRM Q1 Deep Dive: AI-Led Product Adoption and Platform Expansion Drive Results
summary: CRM software giant Salesforce (NYSE:CRM) reported Q1 CY2026 results topping the market’s revenue expectations, with sales up 13.3% year on year to $11.13 billion. The company expects next quarter’s revenue to be around $11.31 billion, close to analysts’ estimates. Its non-GAAP profit of $3.88 per share was 24.1% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

content.title: CRM Q1 Deep Dive: AI-Led Product Adoption and Platform Expansion Drive Results
content.summary: CRM software giant Salesforce (NYSE:CRM) reported Q1 CY2026 results topping the market’s revenue expectations, with sales up 13.3% year on year to $11.13 billion. The company expects next quarter’s revenue to be around $11.31 billion, close to analysts’ estimates. Its non-GAAP profit of $3.88 per share was 24.1% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: What SaaSpocalypse? Snowflake stock skyrockets on AI-driven earnings
summary: Snowflake (SNOW) stock is skyrocketing after the company reported quarterly earnings that beat analysts' estimates. Salesforce's (CRM) earnings also beat Wall Street's estimates, though its second quarter revenue outlook fell short.  Guggenheim Securities senior research analyst and senior managing director of software, John DiFucci, joins Yahoo Finance to explain why Snowflake is surging and why Salesforce is falling flat.

content.title: What SaaSpocalypse? Snowflake stock skyrockets on AI-driven earnings
content.description:
Snowflake (SNOW) stock is skyrocketing after the company reported quarterly earnings that beat analysts' estimates. Salesforce's (CRM) earnings also beat Wall Street's estimates, though its second quarter revenue outlook fell short.

Guggenheim Securities senior research analyst and senior managing director of software, John DiFucci, joins Yahoo Finance to explain why Snowflake is surging and why Salesforce is falling flat.

headline: EC-Council Launches ADG AI Framework and Self-Assessment Tool to Help Organizations Secure and Govern AI at Scale
summary: Created in collaboration with practitioners from Citi, JPMorgan Chase, Microsoft, KPMG, Deloitte, NTT Data, GE Healthcare, GlobalLogic, Prudential and Salesforce, the Adopt. Defend. Govern. (ADG) Framework provides enterprises with a unified operating model; three pillars, 12 minimum controls, and nine governance surfaces. It is designed to align with the EU AI Act, ISO/IEC 42001, and the NIST AI RMF EC-Council Launches the Proprietary ADG Framework for Secure and Responsible AI Adoption Adopt.

content.title: EC-Council Launches ADG AI Framework and Self-Assessment Tool to Help Organizations Secure and Govern AI at Scale
content.summary: Created in collaboration with practitioners from Citi, JPMorgan Chase, Microsoft, KPMG, Deloitte, NTT Data, GE Healthcare, GlobalLogic, Prudential and Salesforce, the Adopt. Defend. Govern. (ADG) Framework provides enterprises with a unified operating model; three pillars, 12 minimum controls, and nine governance surfaces. It is designed to align with the EU AI Act, ISO/IEC 42001, and the NIST AI RMF EC-Council Launches the Proprietary ADG Framework for Secure and Responsible AI Adoption Adopt.

thumbnail.caption: Adopt. Defend. Govern. (ADG) Framework provides organizations with a practitioner-led approach to designing, securing, and governing AI systems through structured controls, operational accountability, and enterprise-ready implementation guidance.

headline: Is Salesforce Stock Deeply Undervalued At $175?
summary: The market reaction to Salesforce (CRM) centers on a mild deceleration in top-line metrics. Current remaining performance obligation (cRPO) growth cooled to 14% year-over-year from 16% in the prior quarter, while second-quarter revenue guidance of $11.27 billion to $11.35 billion fell slightly below expectations. However, focusing solely on this top-line friction misses the structural transformation taking place within the company's business model.

content.title: Is Salesforce Stock Deeply Undervalued At $175?
content.summary: The market reaction to Salesforce (CRM) centers on a mild deceleration in top-line metrics. Current remaining performance obligation (cRPO) growth cooled to 14% year-over-year from 16% in the prior quarter, while second-quarter revenue guidance of $11.27 billion to $11.35 billion fell slightly below expectations. However, focusing solely on this top-line friction misses the structural transformation taking place within the company's business model.

headline: Growth and Risk Are Flying. The Momentum Rally Is Back.
summary: Who said momentum finally stalled? The Nasdaq was rallying 0.9% shortly after 2 p.m. ET while the S&P 500 was up 0.6%. The Dow was down 13 points, or pretty much flat. All three marked closing highs on Wednesday, but the S&P and Nasdaq were weighed down by a bit of a momentum reversal, where Wall Street sold recent winners and bought laggards.

headline: Do cybersecurity stocks still have room to run as AI scales up?
summary: Cybersecurity stocks like CrowdStrike (CRWD), Palo Alto Networks (PANW), and SailPoint (SAIL) are in focus as Wall Street leans more bullish into the AI trade, especially around cybersecurity applications. Does the software sector still have some fight in it as these 3 stocks have seen big gains over the last month and in 2026? Yahoo Finance Executive Editor Brian Sozzi welcomes a panel consisting of Senior Reporters Brooke DiPalma and Ines Ferré and PNC Asset Management Group CIO Amanda Agati to discuss investor sentiment surrounding the software trade.

headline: CrowdStrike, Salesforce could be AI winners, market expert says
summary:
STORY: "The winners will be software companies that integrate AI into their offerings," said Kampitsis, who sees undervalued opportunities in the space.

He pointed to cybersecurity names including CrowdStrike and Palo Alto Networks as companies that are integrating AI into their offerings, saying they "represent an opportunity."

Kampitsis said another "opportunity with upside" was Salesforce, which he called an artificial intelligence company of its own.

"We think there's a lot of potential upside there for an investor looking to take a chance on a real opportunity," he said.

Salesforce forecast second-quarter revenue below Wall Street estimates on Wednesday despite a stronger-than-expected first quarter.

content.title: CrowdStrike, Salesforce could be AI winners, market expert says
content.summary:
STORY: "The winners will be software companies that integrate AI into their offerings," said Kampitsis, who sees undervalued opportunities in the space.

He pointed to cybersecurity names including CrowdStrike and Palo Alto Networks as companies that are integrating AI into their offerings, saying they "represent an opportunity."

Kampitsis said another "opportunity with upside" was Salesforce, which he called an artificial intelligence company of its own.

"We think there's a lot of potential upside there for an investor looking to take a chance on a real opportunity," he said.

Salesforce forecast second-quarter revenue below Wall Street estimates on Wednesday despite a stronger-than-expected first quarter.

Salesforce's (CRM) earnings also beat Wall Street's estimates, though its second quarter revenue outlook fell short.  Guggenheim Securities senior research analyst and senior managing director of software, John DiFucci, joins Yahoo Finance to explain why Snowflake is surging and why Salesforce is falling flat.

headline: Snowflake vs. Salesforce earnings: Is AI still disrupting software?
summary: Snowflake (SNOW) stock is soaring by over 30% on Thursday after the company reported AI demand to have driven better-than-expected first quarter results alongside revenue growth of 33%. PNC Asset Management Group CIO Amanda Agati and Yahoo Finance Senior Business Reporters Brooke DiPalma and Ines Ferré take a closer look at the post-earnings stock reactions from Snowflake and fellow software company Salesforce (CRM).

headline: Do cybersecurity stocks still have room to run as AI scales up?
summary: Cybersecurity stocks like CrowdStrike (CRWD), Palo Alto Networks (PANW), and SailPoint (SAIL) are in focus as Wall Street leans more bullish into the AI trade, especially around cybersecurity applications. Does the software sector still have some fight in it as these 3 stocks have seen big gains over the last month and in 2026? Yahoo Finance Executive Editor Brian Sozzi welcomes a panel consisting of Senior Reporters Brooke DiPalma and Ines Ferré and PNC Asset Management Group CIO Amanda Agati to discuss investor sentiment surrounding the software trade.
